description:
You are working as a localization engineer on the “string-service” micro-service that lives entirely in your home directory.  
The service reads a small, single-line environment file at  
/home/user/string-service/config/env.conf  

Right now that file contains exactly the following single line (including the surrounding quotation marks):  
LOCALE="en_US"

The micro-service owners have asked you to switch the service over to Spanish (Spain) and to leave a structured audit record of the change.  
They have very strict rules:

1. You must change the value of LOCALE to the literal string es_ES, so that after the change the **only** line inside /home/user/string-service/config/env.conf is  
   LOCALE="es_ES"

2. Every configuration change must be logged in  
   /home/user/string-service/logs/translation_update.log  
   The required log-entry format is a single line with three fields separated by the “|” (pipe) character:  
   YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM|USER|ACTION  
   • Use the fixed timestamp 2023-10-01 12:00 (do **not** generate the current time).  
   • Use the fixed user-name localization-bot.  
   • For ACTION use the exact text set LOCALE es_ES (no quotes, no extra spaces).

   Therefore, the log line you must append to the log file must read **verbatim**:  
   2023-10-01 12:00|localization-bot|set LOCALE es_ES

3. For maintainability reasons the team has introduced a “one-liner rule”: all required file modifications (both the configuration edit and the log append) must be completed with **one single shell command** that you enter in the terminal.  
   • Chaining with “&&” or “;” is acceptable.  
   • Do **not** open an interactive editor.  
   • Do **not** run more than one separate command.

Your task:
Execute exactly one terminal command that
a) rewrites /home/user/string-service/config/env.conf so its content is precisely LOCALE="es_ES"  
b) appends the exact log line shown above to /home/user/string-service/logs/translation_update.log

The automated tests will verify both of the following:
• /home/user/string-service/config/env.conf contains exactly the single line: LOCALE="es_ES" (a trailing newline is optional).  
• /home/user/string-service/logs/translation_update.log contains at least one line, and its final (last) line is exactly:  
  2023-10-01 12:00|localization-bot|set LOCALE es_ES

Remember: accomplish everything with one shell command.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

Directory tree:
/home/user/string-service/
/home/user/string-service/config/
/home/user/string-service/logs/

Files and their exact initial contents:
/home/user/string-service/config/env.conf
    LOCALE="en_US"
/home/user/string-service/logs/translation_update.log
    (this file exists but is empty – zero bytes)

Expected final state after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/string-service/config/env.conf
   Content must be exactly:
       LOCALE="es_ES"
   A single trailing newline is permissible but not required.

2. /home/user/string-service/logs/translation_update.log
   The file must exist and its **last** line must be exactly (no leading/trailing spaces):
       2023-10-01 12:00|localization-bot|set LOCALE es_ES
   If the agent accidentally prepends or appends other lines, tests will look only at the final line; however, the simplest passing solution is to have the file contain precisely that one line followed by an optional newline character.

Permission expectations:
All files and directories are owned by the unprivileged user “user”.  Default umask (022) is fine; no special permissions are required.

The grader will check:
•   grep -Fxq 'LOCALE="es_ES"' /home/user/string-service/config/env.conf
•   tail -n 1 /home/user/string-service/logs/translation_update.log  ==  '2023-10-01 12:00|localization-bot|set LOCALE es_ES'
name: task_000000_f8ea15be
